name: logilab-database
documentation:
logilab-database provides some classes to make unified access to different
RDBMS possible:

* actually compatible db-api from different drivers to postgres, mysql,
  sqlite and sqlserver
* additional api for full text search
* extensions functions for common tasks such as creating database, index,
  users, dump and restore, etc...
* sql generation for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (in sqlgen.py)

functions.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.name_fields
documentation:
Take a cursor and a list of records fetched with that cursor, then return a
list of dictionaries (one for each record) whose keys are column names and
values are records' values.

```
param cursor

cursor used to execute the query

param records

list returned by fetch*()
```

classes.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLExpression
documentation:
Use this class when you need direct SQL expression in statements
generated by SQLGenerator. Arguments:

- a sqlstring that defines the SQL expression to be used, e.g. 'YEARS(%(date)s)'

- kwargs that define the values to be substituted in the SQL expression,
  e.g. date='2013/01/01'

E.g. the SQL expression SQLExpression('YEARS(%(date)s)', date='2013/01/01')
will yield:

'..., age = YEARS(%(date)s), ...' in a SQL statement

and will modify accordingly the parameters:

{'date': '2013/01/01', ...}

classes.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator._iterate_params
documentation:
Iterate a parameters dictionnary and yield the correct column name
and value (base types or SQL functions)

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.where
documentation:
---
param addon: additional sql statement
param keys: list of keys
---

```pycon
>>> s = SQLGenerator()
>>> s.where(['nom'])
'nom = %(nom)s'
>>> s.where(['nom','prenom'])
'nom = %(nom)s AND prenom = %(prenom)s'
>>> s.where(['nom','prenom'], 'x.id = y.id')
'x.id = y.id AND nom = %(nom)s AND prenom = %(prenom)s'
```

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.set
documentation:
---
param keys: list of keys
---

```pycon
>>> s = SQLGenerator()
>>> s.set(['nom'])
'nom = %(nom)s'
>>> s.set(['nom','prenom'])
'nom = %(nom)s, prenom = %(prenom)s'
```

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.insert
documentation:
---
param params: dictionary that will be used as in cursor.execute(sql,params)
param table: name of the table
---

```pycon
>>> s = SQLGenerator()
>>> s.insert('test',{'nom':'dupont'})
'INSERT INTO test ( nom ) VALUES ( %(nom)s )'
>>> params = {'nom':'dupont', 'prenom':'jean',
...          'age': SQLExpression('YEARS(%(date)s)', date='2013/01/01')}
>>> s.insert('test', params)
'INSERT INTO test ( age, nom, prenom ) VALUES ( YEARS(%(date)s), %(nom)s, %(prenom)s )'
>>> params['date'] # params has been modified
'2013/01/01'
```

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.select
documentation:
---
param params: dictionary that will be used as in cursor.execute(sql,params)
param table: name of the table
---

```pycon
>>> s = SQLGenerator()
>>> s.select('test',{})
'SELECT * FROM test'
>>> s.select('test',{'nom':'dupont'})
'SELECT * FROM test WHERE nom = %(nom)s'
>>> s.select('test',{'nom':'dupont','prenom':'jean'})
'SELECT * FROM test WHERE nom = %(nom)s AND prenom = %(prenom)s'
```

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.adv_select
documentation:
---
param joins: |-
  optional list of restriction statements to insert in the
  where clause. Usually used to perform joins.
param model: list of columns to select
param params: dictionary that will be used as in cursor.execute(sql, params)
param tables: list of tables used in from
---

```pycon
>>> s = SQLGenerator()
>>> s.adv_select(['column'],[('test', 't')], {})
'SELECT column FROM test AS t'
>>> s.adv_select(['column'],[('test', 't')], {'nom':'dupont'})
'SELECT column FROM test AS t WHERE nom = %(nom)s'
```

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.delete
documentation:
---
param params: dictionary that will be used as in cursor.execute(sql,params)
param table: name of the table
---

```pycon
>>> s = SQLGenerator()
>>> s.delete('test',{'nom':'dupont'})
'DELETE FROM test WHERE nom = %(nom)s'
>>> s.delete('test',{'nom':'dupont','prenom':'jean'})
'DELETE FROM test WHERE nom = %(nom)s AND prenom = %(prenom)s'
```

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.delete_many
documentation: Delete many using the IN clause

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlgen.SQLGenerator.update
documentation:
---
param params: dictionary that will be used as in cursor.execute(sql,params)
param table: name of the table
---

```pycon
>>> s = SQLGenerator()
>>> s.update('test', {'id':'001','nom':'dupont'}, ['id'])
'UPDATE test SET nom = %(nom)s WHERE id = %(id)s'
>>> s.update('test',{'id':'001','nom':'dupont','prenom':'jean'},['id'])
'UPDATE test SET nom = %(nom)s, prenom = %(prenom)s WHERE id = %(id)s'
```
Helper class to generate SQL strings to use with python's DB-API.

methods.name: logilab.database.ftiquery.Query.execute
documentation:
execute this query using the given cursor
yield a list of 2-uple (rating, uid)

a query is the object manipulated by the indexer
the query parser'll call add_word and add_phrase on this object accoring to
the query string (see query.g for the query string's grammar)

execute this query using the given cursor

a keywords query'll look for uid matching all those words in any order

documentation:
execute this query using the given cursor
the query maybe restricted to a given list of uids

return a dict with uid as keys and rating as value
a phrase query'll look for uid matching all phrase's tokens in the same order

methods.name: logilab.database.sqlite._Sqlite3Adapter._init_module
documentation:
This declares adapters for the sqlite _module_
and should be called only once.

We do this here in order to be as lazy as possible
(these adapters won't be added until we instantiate at
least one Sqlite3Adapter object).

modules.name: logilab.database
documentation:
Wrappers to get actually replaceable DBAPI2 compliant modules and
database connection whatever the database and client lib used.

Currently support:

- postgresql (pgdb, psycopg, psycopg2, pyPgSQL)
- sqlite (pysqlite2, sqlite, sqlite3)

just use the `get_connection` function from this module to get a
wrapped connection.  If multiple drivers for a database are available,
you can control which one you want to use using the
`set_prefered_driver` function.

Additional helpers are also provided for advanced functionalities such
as listing existing users or databases, creating database... Get the
helper for your database using the `get_db_helper` function.
copyright 2003-2014 LOGILAB S.A. (Paris, FRANCE), all rights reserved.
contact http://www.logilab.fr/ -- mailto:contact@logilab.fr

functions.name: logilab.database.set_prefered_driver
documentation:
sets the preferred driver module for driver
driver is the name of the db engine (postgresql,...)
module is the name of the module providing the connect function
syntax is (params_func, post_process_func_or_None)
_drivers is a optional dictionary of drivers

functions.name: logilab.database._import_driver_module
documentation:
Imports the first module found in 'drivers' for 'driver'

```
rtype

tuple

returns

the tuple module_object, module_name where module_object
is the dbapi module, and modname the module's name
```

methods.name: logilab.database.DBAPIAdapter.process_cursor
documentation:
return an iterator on results.

Each record is returned a list (not a tuple) and each element
of the record is processed :
- database  strings are all unicode
- database booleans are python boolean objects
- if `binarywrap` is provided, it is used to wrap binary data

methods.name: logilab.database.DBAPIAdapter._transformations
documentation:
returns the set of required transformations on the resultset

Transformations are the functions used to convert raw results as
returned by the dbapi module to standard python objects (e.g.
unicode, bool, etc.)

methods.name: logilab.database._GenericAdvFuncHelper.backup_commands
documentation:
Return a list of commands to backup the given database.

Each command may be given as a list or as a string. In the latter case,
expected to be used with a subshell (for instance using `os.system(cmd)`
or `subprocess.call(cmd, shell=True)`

methods.name: logilab.database._GenericAdvFuncHelper.restore_commands
documentation:
Return a list of commands to restore a backup of the given database.


Each command may be given as a list or as a string. In the latter case,
expected to be used with a subshell (for instance using `os.system(cmd)`
or `subprocess.call(cmd, shell=True)`

methods.name: logilab.database._GenericAdvFuncHelper.sql_current_date

Return sql for the current date.

Take care default implementation return date at the beginning of the
transaction on some backend (eg postgres)
helpers to standardize SQL according to the database #####################

methods.name: logilab.database._GenericAdvFuncHelper.sql_add_order_by
documentation:
add an ORDER BY clause to the SQL query, and wrap the query if necessary
:sql: the original sql query
:sortterms: a list of tuples with term, sorting order and nulls policy
:selection: the selection that must be gathered after ORDER BY
:needwrap: boolean, True if the query must be wrapped in a subquery
:has_limit_or_offset: not used (sqlserver helper needs this)

methods.name: logilab.database._GenericAdvFuncHelper.temporary_table_name

functions.name: logilab.database.fti.normalize
documentation:
Return the normalized form for a word.

The word given in argument should be unicode !

currently normalized word are :
   _ in lower case
   _ without any accent

This function may raise StopWord if the word shouldn't be indexed

stop words are :
   _ single letter

modules.name: logilab.database.postgres
documentation:
Postgres RDBMS support

Supported drivers, in order of preference:
- psycopg2
- psycopg2ct

Full-text search based on the tsearch2 extension from the openfts project
(see http://openfts.sourceforge.net/)

Warning: you will need to run the tsearch2.sql script with super user privileges
on the database.

cf. https://pypi.python.org/pypi/psycopg2cffi

classes.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.system_database
documentation: return the system database for the given driver

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.sql_regexp_match_expression
documentation: pattern matching using regexp

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.sql_add_order_by
documentation:
add an ORDER BY clause to the SQL query, and wrap the query if necessary
:sql: the original sql query
:sortterms: a list of tuples with term, sorting order and nulls policy
:selection: the selection that must be gathered after ORDER BY
:needwrap: boolean, True if the query must be wrapped in a subquery
:has_limit_or_offset: not used (sqlserver helper needs this)

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.create_database
documentation: create a new database

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.create_schema
documentation: create a new database schema

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.drop_schema
documentation: drop a database schema

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.create_language
documentation: postgres specific method to install a procedural language on a database

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.list_users
documentation: return the list of existing database users

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.list_databases
documentation: return the list of existing databases

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.list_tables
documentation: return the list of tables of a database

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.list_indices
documentation: return the list of indices of a database, only for the given table if specified

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.cursor_index_object
documentation: Index an object, using the db pointed by the given cursor.

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.fulltext_search
documentation: Execute a full text query and return a list of 2-uple (rating, uid).

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.fti_restriction_sql
documentation: Execute a full text query and return a list of 2-uple (rating, uid).

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.fti_rank_order
documentation: Execute a full text query and return a list of 2-uple (rating, uid).

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.find_tsearch2_schema
documentation: Looks up for tsearch2.sql in a list of default paths.
XXX not needed with postgres >= 8.3 right?

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.init_fti_extensions
documentation:
If necessary, install extensions at database creation time.

For postgres, install tsearch2 if not installed by the template.

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.sql_init_fti
documentation:
Return the sql definition of table()s used by the full text index.

Require extensions to be already in.

methods.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.sql_drop_fti
documentation: Drop tables used by the full text index.

class_variables.name: logilab.database.postgres._PGAdvFuncHelper.max_indexed
documentation: 500KB, avoid "string is too long for tsvector"
Postgres helper, taking advantage of postgres SEQUENCE support
